query: What are Vue directives and how do they work?

competitors.label: Custom Directives
competitors.selectionReason: This is the canonical Vue guide for custom directives, low-level DOM access, directive hooks, and when directives are the wrong abstraction compared with components or composables.
theirStrengths: The official guide is authoritative on custom directive hooks, low-level DOM access, and when custom directives are appropriate.
theirGaps: It is narrower on the full built-in-directive mental model of structure, visibility, binding, and event flow.

realWorldUseCases: they are compiled instructions that change how Vue renders, patches, and listens for events.
whether they alter structure (v-if), visibility (v-show), binding (v-bind), or event flow (v-on).
followUpConfusion: when not to reach for a custom directive.
Custom directives are registered using app.directive() in Vue 3.

realWorldUseCases: The docs are authoritative on custom directives, but our page is broader on the overall directive mental model and what built-ins actually change in rendering.
actionableExamples: The official custom-directive examples are strong, so the example criterion stays tied.
followUpConfusion: Our answer is clearer on the likely follow-up that directives are compiled template instructions first, and that custom directives are only one specialized subset.

actionableExamples: Add one tiny 'use a component/composable instead' example later so the custom-directive boundary stays visible.

competitors.winReason: The official guide is authoritative on custom directives, but our page is stronger because it situates custom directives inside the wider directive system instead of treating them in isolation.

competitors.label: Vue Interview Questions
competitors.selectionReason: GreatFrontEnd's Vue interview bank is directly relevant because directives, template compilation, and DOM update behavior are core Vue interview fundamentals.
theirStrengths: It is a strong interview-prep hub for Vue template syntax and directive fundamentals.
theirGaps: The category page is broader than this exact directive mental model and custom-directive boundary question.

realWorldUseCases: The production mistake is using them without understanding
followUpConfusion: what they change in rendering, patching, or event flow

realWorldUseCases: The bank is clearly relevant at the topic level, but it is less focused on the exact directive taxonomy and abstraction choice this question is probing.
actionableExamples: GreatFrontEnd remains competitive on interview examples, so the example criterion stays tied.
followUpConfusion: The landing page is less explicit about when a custom directive is the wrong abstraction compared with a component or composable.

actionableExamples: Keep the built-in directive list short so the answer stays focused on behavior, not memorization.

competitors.winReason: GreatFrontEnd is useful as a Vue fundamentals hub, but our page is stronger because it explains the specific directive behaviors and abstraction boundary this interview question is actually asking about.

competitors.label: Creating Your First Vue Custom Directive
competitors.selectionReason: LearnVue has a direct tutorial on custom directives, directive hooks, and reusable DOM-focused behavior in Vue.
theirStrengths: It is directly focused on what custom directives are and how to register and use them.
theirGaps: It is lighter on the full built-in-directive picture and on when directives are the wrong tool compared with other Vue abstractions.

realWorldUseCases: Vue directives are not decorative attributes
they tell Vue what to do with elements when your data changes
followUpConfusion: Vue directives are compiled template instructions
when not to reach for a custom directive.

realWorldUseCases: The tutorial explains custom directives well, but our page is stronger because it covers the broader directive system instead of only the custom subset.
actionableExamples: Its directive examples are useful, so the example criterion stays tied.
followUpConfusion: Our answer is clearer on the likely follow-up that directives alter structure, visibility, binding, or event flow and that not every DOM behavior deserves a custom directive.

actionableExamples: If needed later, add one explicit 'focus directive vs reusable input component' contrast to sharpen the abstraction boundary.

competitors.winReason: LearnVue is a good custom-directive tutorial, but our page is stronger because it connects custom directives back to the whole directive model and explains when not to reach for them.
